text: NEW: Two ships from Iran are crossing the Strait of Hormuz despite the US blockade. Source: Kpler intelligence

Qatar’s Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Majed al-Ansari: The attacks on our country persisted and only stopped with the ceasefire. There have been absolutely no money discussions on this. Any talk of financially stopping these attacks is completely false and just aims to tarnish the reputation of my country.

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 According to Aviation Weekly, quoting Army spokesperson Jimmy Arter, the United States has deployed all stocks of the latest high-precision ground-to-air ballistic PrSM (Precision Strike Missile) missiles since the start of military operations against Iran. This type of weapon is designed to replace the old ATACMS systems and offers greater range and accuracy.

🇮🇹 ❌ 🇮🇱 Reuters: Italy's government has decided to ​suspend the automatic renewal of a defence ‌cooperation deal with Israel, Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said on Tuesday, citing the conflicts in the Middle East. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇫🇷 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇱🇧 🇬🇧 French President Emmanuel Macron said He spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and U.S. President Donald Trump, calling for the resumption of negotiations that broke down in Islamabad and for the ceasefire to be strictly respected by all, including Lebanon. He urged the unconditional reopening of the Strait of Hormuz without controls or tolls. He stated that France and the United Kingdom will host a videoconference on Friday in Paris with non‑belligerent countries ready to contribute to a multilateral defensive mission to restore freedom of navigation in the strait once security conditions allow. @wfwitness

Open Source Intel “Bechadarei” reports Ultra Orthodox analysts played a key intelligence role in the IDF strike that eliminated ~250 Hezbollah commanders and operatives in 10 minutes in Lebanon. Graduates of the “Pardes” program, which trains Ultra Orthodox men in intelligence analysis and advanced data processing, they helped process months of accumulated and real time intelligence. Their work enabled precise tracking of dozens of targets and a highly coordinated, near simultaneous strike. Image: Avratech director Aaron Safrai (right) with Ultra Orthodox students learning data processing and programming at the company’s Jerusalem offices. (Photo: Marc Israel Sellem) tweet

OSINTdefender According to Reuters, Indonesian Foreign Ministry sent a letter to the Indonesian Defense Ministry warning against granting blanket flyover permission to U.S. military flights, citing the risk of friction with China in the South China Sea region. The letter was delivered prior to the meeting between U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Indonesian Defense Minister Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin in Washington yesterday. tweet

Serbia plans to jointly produce combat drones with Israel, President Aleksandar Vucic said. “We will be doing it together… 50-50… and we will have the best drones in this part of the world,” he stated, adding that Serbia alone “cannot make drones like Israel.”

We did not believe the mission would be finished immediately after the fighting subsided, but we did plan—indeed carefully—that our campaign would continue and be expressed even in the period following the strikes in Tehran.

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: Fwd from @ 📝 Close the Strait of Hormuz — open Bab el-Mandeb? 📝 tensions rising in Riyadh Against the backdrop of the blockade of Iranian ports launched by Trump , Saudi Arabia started pressuring Washington : the kingdom is demanding to abandon this scheme and return to the negotiating table. ➡️ Riyadh fears that in response to the pressure, the Iranians could block Bab el-Mandeb — a key flow of oil and cargo to Europe through the Suez Canal passes through it. 🖍 For the Saudis, this story is not new. After the start of the war, a significant portion of exports was redirected from the Persian Gulf to the Red Sea — to the port of Yanbu, through which they currently manage to maintain a supply volume of around 7 million barrels per day. If Bab el-Mandeb also comes under fire, this route will simply stop working — the kingdom has no alternatives at such volumes. 🚩 And the risks here are quite obvious. Yemeni Houthis, who control the coast near the strait, have already shown they can strike shipping — during the Gaza conflict, the flow dropped by almost half. And after the official entry of Ansarallah into the war with ballistic missile strikes on Israel , the risks of blocking have only increased . ❗️ In addition, there remains the option of repeating the "Iranian scheme" — introducing passage fees or restrictions on vessels. 📌 As a result, an attempt to apply pressure through one strait risks turning into problems in two places at once. And then the question will no longer be how to open the Strait of Hormuz, but how not to lose the Red Sea. #Iran #SaudiArabia #USA 🧨 @rybar_mena — on Middle Eastern chaos with love 💸 Support us Original msg

Hillary Clinton: I know from personal experience how Netanyahu has tried to get every American president to agree to have an open-ended war with Iran. I had many, many long, hours-long conversations with him and his war cabinet about this, and refusing to go along with a very inchoate desire to do something to Iran with no real end state that could be described sufficiently.

Hillary Clinton: It's shocking that Trump said nobody told him that Iran might close the Strait of Hormuz. Every war game I was ever involved in, that was the first thing we assumed Iran would do. We're in a situation now where we are weak, where we essentially have lost the leverage and initiative that we had.

🇺🇸 🇭🇺 JD Vance on his failed mission in Hungary to support Viktor Orbán: We didn’t go to Hungary because we expected Viktor Orbán to cruise to an election victory; we went because we thought it was the right thing to do.
Zelensky: We don’t want “NATO light” or “EU light.” We want proper membership in the EU.

Zelensky: We’re talking about, in particular, air defense against ballistic missiles. There are simply not enough production capacities in Europe. We need to ensure that Ukraine works with the EU to produce these anti-ballistic missile defense systems.

German Chancellor Merz on Ukraine’s EU accession: It’s not going to be second-class membership. It’s a process where we bring them closer, and there is homework that Ukraine has to do. We have accession criteria.
⚡️ 🇴🇲 The UKTMO reports that they have received a report of two unidentified projectiles striking a vessel 112 nautical miles southeast of Ras al Hadd, Oman, on April 7th. @wfwitness
Zelensky on drone deal with Germany: This is going to be the largest deal of its kind in Europe.
Zelensky: We will be producing anti-ballistic missile systems to protect us against any kind of drones, any kinds of aerial attacks. And we are working together to achieve this result.
German Chancellor Merz: Russia should end this war as quickly as possible. It has no chance of winning it.

German Chancellor Merz: We are going to step up our measures against Russia’s shadow fleet. We will also be working together on the Middle East in order to increase defense capabilities and organize this better.

German Chancellor Merz on Hungary election results: They didn’t just vote to get rid of one government, but rather to get rid of a regime. Since 1989, they have once again voted for freedom. The Hungarians have chosen Europe and the world. Impressively, there is no trend that cannot be stopped when it comes to right-wing populism and autocracy. So the pendulum has swung back the other way, which is good news for Europe, good news for Germany, and also good news for Ukraine.

German Chancellor Merz: The number of Ukrainian men who have taken refuge is something we will be working on with Ukraine to limit, because it is crucial that these men are there to help their country.
German Chancellor Merz: We elevate our bilateral relations with Ukraine to the level of a strategic partnership.
